text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy little town of Willow Creek there lived a rabbit named Holly. She’d hop everywhere dreaming of big adventures.

One windy autumn day Holly decided she’d chase the falling leaves downtown. It seemed thrilling. She hurdled mogwi mushrooms here, piranhabenoes there! Eventually she found herself near a cozy little bookstore.

Inside they welcomed her like old friends. “We’ve barely got any leaves!” murmured the bookworm.

“Well,” pondered Holly, “maybe I’ll leave them a little treasure.”

She picked out a rare tome on fox folklore. They loved it!

Back home Holly thought how cool tales must feel.

On Christmas Eve Holly listened. Her soft nose twitched.

There was a fox who knew snow angels.

She followed him at night.

Later under a frosty moon.

He danced fox fashion.

Holly watched wide-eyed.

Eventually that fox spoke.

“In cold times feast!”

Holly found berries wild.

They feasted till dawn.

She
